page: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type, though it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark data places the processor performance in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests. The AMD EPYC CPU includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptography acceleration via AES and VAES. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks rank in the top 10%, while the Passmark memory score is in the top 25%. In application-specific workloads, the instance achieves top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and Redis SET operations. Large language model (LLM) inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing speeds, though text generation speeds range from average for small models to strong for medium and large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8a.48xlarge offers high compute density and memory speed relative to its hardware class, though the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage attachments for data-intensive operations. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff that prioritizes raw processing power over massive in-memory databases. This configuration is suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, cryptography, and batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place its single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation capabilities in the top tier (top 10%). While prompt processing for large language models ranks in the top tier, text generation performance ranges from average to strong. This instance is configured for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, static web serving, and high-throughput batch processing where high CPU density is required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 192 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, DDR5 memory, and top-tier processing performance for intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and software compilation benchmark results
No local storage or GPU hardware included in the configuration
Suited for high-throughput batch processing, cryptography, and web serving
